subject: Biology
title: Marine Snow: Food from Above
description: A quick, practical guide to Marine Snow: Food from Above.

hook.text: In the deep ocean, there's no sunlight and no plants. All food comes from above—falling like snow.

buildup.text: Marine snow is a constant shower of dead plankton, fecal matter, and organic debris from the surface.

discovery.text: It takes weeks for a particle to sink from the surface to the abyssal floor, thousands of meters below.

twist.text: Whale falls—dead whales sinking to the bottom—create entire ecosystems that can last for decades.

climax.text: Deep-sea life depends entirely on this slow rain of death from above. No snow, no life.

punchline.text: In the deep, dinner is whatever drifts down from the surface.

quiz.question: What is marine snow?
options: Dead organic matter falling from the surface to the deep ocean
Frozen seawater crystals at the poles
